title: Gas spring and bridge for a heat pump
abstract: A gas-fired heat pump that has independently-actuated displacers, as opposed to crank coupled displacers, has some displacer strokes that require more energy than others to travel. By coupling the displacers by a spring, the forces to move the displacers are more evenly matched. A gas spring coupled between the displacers is disclosed in which volume with the gas spring includes: a portion of the volume within the hot displacer, a portion of the volume within the cold displacer, and a volume between the two displacers. Also disclosed is a bridge that spans across the housing between the hot and cold displacers. The forces acting on the displacers via the gas spring are based on pressure in the gas spring and the cross-sectional areas of plungers on the hot displacer, the cold displacer, and the bridge.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A heat pump, comprising: first and second cylinders arranged collinearly; a hot displacer disposed within the first cylinder and adapted to reciprocate within the first cylinder portion; a cold displacer disposed with the second cylinder and adapted to reciprocate with the second cylinder portion; a perforated bridge clamped between the first and second cylinder; and a gas spring disposed between the hot and the cold displacers. 2. The heat pump of claim 1 wherein: a hot displacer portion of the gas spring volume is contained within the hot displacer; a cold displacer portion of the gas spring volume is contained within the cold displacer; and volume within the gas spring varies in response to movement of the hot and cold displacers. 3. The heat pump of claim 1, wherein: the bridge passes through the warm chamber defining a warm hot chamber on a side of the bridge proximate the hot displacer and a warm cold chamber on a side of the bridge proximate the cold displacer; and perforations on the bridge allow flow between the warm hot chamber and the warm cold chamber. 4. The heat pump of claim 1 wherein: the bridge has a plunger cylinder extending from the bridge toward the cold displacer, the plunger cylinder having a first predetermined inside diameter and a second predetermined outer diameter. 5. The heat pump of claim 4 wherein: the hot displacer has a centrally-located, hot- displacer plunger with an outer diameter that is slightly smaller than the first predetermined inside diameter of the plunger cylinder of the bridge so that when the 

 hot displacer reciprocates within the first cylinder, the hot-displacer plunger reciprocates within the plunger cylinder of the bridge. 6. The heat pump of claim 4 wherein: the cold displacer surface has an internal cylinder proximate the bridge with an inside diameter slightly larger than the second predetermined outer diameter of the plunger cylinder of the bridge so that the internal cylinder moves over the plunger cylinder of the bridge when the cold displacer reciprocates. 7. The heat pump of claim 6 wherein at least one orifice is provided through a wall of the internal cylinder of the cold displacer to provide fluidic communication between gases inside the internal cylinder and gases outside the internal cylinder that are enclosed in the cold displacer. 8. The heat pump of claim 6, further comprising: first and second circumferential grooves defined in a surface of the plunger cylinder of the bridge; a bearing disposed in the first groove; and a seal disposed in the second groove wherein the hot-displacer plunger rides on the bearing and the seal when the hot displacer reciprocates. 9. The heat pump of claim 4, the plunger cylinder is one of: integrally formed with the bridge and affixed to the bridge. 10. The heat pump of claim 4 wherein: the gas spring has a volume that includes: a portion of the internal volume of the hot displacer; a portion of the internal volume of the cold displacer; and a portion of the volume within the plunger cylinder of the bridge.
